ci-test-fee-practice.title: Test polymorphic fees
ci-test-fee-practice.prompt: Write assertions for two different fee objects and their combined service total.
ci-test-fee-practice.hint: Read the imports first, then put each responsibility in the file named by that import.

help.concept: In a multi-file project, each file should own one clear responsibility and imports should connect those responsibilities.
help.hint_1: Run from the entry file and follow the first failing import or assertion.
help.hint_2: Keep the full project state working, not just the one line you changed.

ci-test-fee-practice.starterCode:
import sys
sys.path.insert(0, ".")

from models.book_fee import BookFee
from models.video_fee import VideoFee
from services.fees import total_fees

items = [BookFee(2), VideoFee(3)]
# add assertions
print("fee tests passed")

sc-testing-inheritance-and-polymorphism-.title: Check the design choice
sc-testing-inheritance-and-polymorphism-.prompt: Which choice best matches the lesson goal for testing inheritance and polymorphism?
sc-testing-inheritance-and-polymorphism-.hint: Choose the option that keeps object responsibilities clear.

help.concept: Verify subclasses, overrides, and mixed lists so the design stays safe while it grows.
help.hint_1: Look for the answer that reduces duplication without hiding behavior.
help.hint_2: A good OOP answer usually keeps behavior near the object that owns the data.

options.a: Put every class in main.py so imports are never needed.
options.b: Give each class or service a focused responsibility and import it where needed.
options.c: Avoid methods so every object exposes all of its data directly.
options.d: Copy the same logic into each subclass to avoid shared code.

try-testing-inheritance-and-polymorphism.hint: Read the imports first, then put each responsibility in the file named by that import.

help.concept: In a multi-file project, each file should own one clear responsibility and imports should connect those responsibilities.

try-testing-inheritance-and-polymorphism.starterCode:
import sys
sys.path.insert(0, ".")

from models.bug_ticket import BugTicket

ticket = BugTicket("Crash", "high")
# assert the specialized label
print("bug ticket tests passed")

try-testing-inheritance-and-polymorphism.title: Test a mixed object service
try-testing-inheritance-and-polymorphism.prompt: Assert that `render_report()` works with different objects that share `render()`.
try-testing-inheritance-and-polymorphism.hint: Read the imports first, then put each responsibility in the file named by that import.

help.concept: In a multi-file project, each file should own one clear responsibility and imports should connect those responsibilities.
help.hint_1: Run from the entry file and follow the first failing import or assertion.
help.hint_2: Keep the full project state working, not just the one line you changed.

try-testing-inheritance-and-polymorphism.starterCode:
import sys
sys.path.insert(0, ".")

from models.metric_item import MetricItem
from models.text_item import TextItem
from services.reports import render_report

items = [MetricItem("Open", 2), TextItem("Ready")]
# assert exact report text
print("report tests passed")

concept.title: Testing Inheritance and Polymorphism: the idea
concept.bodyMarkdown: **Testing Inheritance and Polymorphism** is not just syntax. In this course it is practiced inside small projects where one file owns one clear part of the program. Verify subclasses, overrides, and mixed lists so the design stays safe while it grows.

multifile-shape.title: Testing Inheritance and Polymorphism: the file shape
multifile-shape.bodyMarkdown: The file layout matters. Models hold object state and behavior, services coordinate groups of objects, tests verify behavior, and `main.py` stays thin enough to read.

graded-habit.title: Testing Inheritance and Polymorphism: what the grader checks
graded-habit.bodyMarkdown: The grader checks behavior from the running project. A strong answer keeps imports working, keeps all files present, and proves the object behavior through output or assertions.
